rationale: The cited paper is used in the citing paper's construct-definition section to anchor what their WTP measure corresponds to (the floor reservation price in Wang et al. 2007). The authors then explicitly qualify that they do not adopt Wang et al.'s range conceptualization and note that its procedure had only been implemented for direct elicitation, not indirect conjoint-style methods. This citation therefore plays a meaningful framing and scope-setting role, not just a background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper references Wang et al. (2007) to position its WTP definition relative to the range-based reservation-price framework, then narrows its own analysis to point WTP (floor price) for comparability and method coverage. It uses Wang et al. as a conceptual foil to delimit the study's assumptions and measurement approach.
